ideal_must_detect.matched_text: we do not collect personal phone numbers for onboarding

must_detect.matched_text:
North Albion Capital plc — DPA Schedule (HR, Onboarding, and Market‑Abuse Compliance) — United Kingdom
Controller: North Albion Capital plc (UK issuer admitted to trading on the North Albion Exchange (NAEX)); Processor: Greyhaven Data Systems Ltd (HRIS/payroll).
Lawful bases: contract and legal obligation for employment records; legal obligation for UK MAR insider‑list maintenance; consent only for optional wellbeing surveys.
Categories: role email, employee number, office location code (e.g., LDN‑01), right‑to‑work evidence reference, training completion status; we do not collect personal phone numbers for onboarding.
Data minimisation: onboarding forms v2.3 require only mandatory role data; market surveillance logs capture header metadata by default and exclude message content unless an escalation is approved.
Retention: recruitment records are held 12 months then anonymised; insider lists are retained for five years from the date the relevant information is made public; generic deal‑room access logs are held 24 months.
Data subject rights: requests are accepted via https://privacy.northalbioncapital.example/dsar or the mailbox privacy@northalbioncapital.example; verification uses role‑based checks rather than government IDs.
DSAR/erasure handling: On 06 May 2026 we completed a DSAR and associated erasure for a former analyst; response logged as DSAR‑2026‑077 and audit‑complete.
Consent withdrawal: employees may withdraw consent to non‑essential wellbeing surveys at any time via the portal; no active withdrawals are pending as at the date of this note.
Cross‑border transfer controls: UK IDTA and Transfer Risk Assessment for Greyhaven’s Calidora support centre were approved on 15 Jan 2026 (Approval Ref NAEX‑DXR‑2026‑014); no export runs are scheduled this week.
Security and access: data are encrypted at rest and in transit; insider‑list access is restricted to Compliance; deal documentation is stored in a segregated workspace with least‑privilege permissions.
MNPI handling: MNPI is processed strictly for insider lists and wall‑crossing records under UK MAR; training materials use stale or publicly announced scenarios and do not include live transaction de
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
